summary: Recent updates to Firefox's WebVTT (Web Video Text Tracks) implementation, which is the technology used for displaying captions and subtitles in videos, have improved how it processes content. By switching from using `innerHTML` to `setHTML`, this change enhances security and performance when rendering caption text. Additionally, incorporating `MOZ_LOG_FMT` for logging purposes allows developers to better track problems related to captions, ultimately making video playback smoother and more reliable for users. These enhancements are crucial as they contribute to a more robust and polished viewing experience.
